## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## ✖ `sigma_O` is smaller than 0.01
## ℹ Consider omitting this part of the model
## ✖ `sigma_E` is smaller than 0.01
## ℹ Consider omitting this part of the model
## ✔ Range parameters don't look unreasonably large
# Predictions on training and test data
training_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = train_data, type = "response")
training_pred$observed <- train_data$d_scaled

test_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
test_pred$observed <- test_data$d_scaled

# Plotting the predictions
p0 <- ggplot(training_pred, aes(x = observed, y = est)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  ggtitle("Train") + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, col = "red")

p1 <- ggplot(test_pred, aes(x = observed, y = est)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  ggtitle("Test") + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, col = "red")

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p0, p1)

# Compute RMSE
rmse_train <- rmse(training_pred$observed, training_pred$est)
rmse_test <- rmse(test_pred$observed, test_pred$est)

# Compute Range
range_train <- range(train_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)
range_test <- range(test_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)

# Compute Standard Deviation
sd_train <- sd(train_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)
sd_test <- sd(test_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)

# Assemble the table
summary_table <- data.frame(
  Dataset = c("Training", "Test"),
  RMSE = c(rmse_train, rmse_test),
  Range_Min = c(range_train[1], range_test[1]),
  Range_Max = c(range_train[2], range_test[2]),
  Std_Dev = c(sd_train, sd_test)
)

# Print the summary table
print(summary_table)
##    Dataset      RMSE Range_Min Range_Max   Std_Dev
## 1 Training 0.7246156         0 31.976570 1.2961276
## 2     Test 0.6989407         0  7.145901 0.6932196

tem&salinity with rw

# Create mesh using training data
mesh_train <- make_mesh(train_data,
                        xy_cols = c("X", "Y"),
                        cutoff = 5)

# Fit spatiotemporal model
fit_spatiotemporal <- sdmTMB(
  d_scaled ~ temperature_depth_55_std + salinity_depth_55_std,
  data = train_data, 
  mesh = mesh_train,
  time = "season",
  family = delta_gamma(link1 = "cloglog", link2 = "log"), 
  spatial = "on", 
  spatiotemporal = "rw",
  extra_time = unique(test_data$season)
)
## Detected irregular time spacing with an AR(1) or random walk process.
## Consider filling in the missing time slices with `extra_time`.
## `extra_time = c(2018, 2020, 2021, 2022)`
## Warning: The model may not have converged: non-positive-definite Hessian
## matrix.
# Extract coefficients and sanity check
#tidy(fit_spatiotemporal, conf.int = TRUE)
sanity(fit_spatiotemporal)
## ✔ Non-linear minimizer suggests successful convergence
## ✖ Non-positive-definite Hessian matrix: model may not have converged
## ℹ Try simplifying the model, adjusting the mesh, or adding priors
## ✔ No extreme or very small eigenvalues detected
## ✔ No gradients with respect to fixed effects are >= 0.001
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## ✔ No fixed-effect standard errors are NA
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## ✖ `ln_tau_O` standard error may be large
## ℹ `ln_tau_O` is an internal parameter affecting `sigma_O`
## ℹ `sigma_O` is the spatial standard deviation
## ℹ Try simplifying the model, adjusting the mesh, or adding priors
## ✖ `ln_tau_E` standard error may be large
## ℹ `ln_tau_E` is an internal parameter affecting `sigma_E`
## ℹ `sigma_E` is the spatiotemporal standard deviation
## ℹ Try simplifying the model, adjusting the mesh, or adding priors
## ✖ `ln_kappa` standard error may be large
## ℹ `ln_kappa` is an internal parameter affecting `range`
## ℹ `range` is the distance at which data are effectively independent
## ℹ Try simplifying the model, adjusting the mesh, or adding priors
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## ✖ `sigma_E` is smaller than 0.01
## ℹ Consider omitting this part of the model
## ✖ `sigma_O` is smaller than 0.01
## ℹ Consider omitting this part of the model
## ✖ `sigma_E` is smaller than 0.01
## ℹ Consider omitting this part of the model
## ✔ Range parameters don't look unreasonably large
# Predictions on training and test data
training_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = train_data, type = "response")
training_pred$observed <- train_data$d_scaled

test_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
test_pred$observed <- test_data$d_scaled

# Plotting the predictions
p0 <- ggplot(training_pred, aes(x = observed, y = est)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  ggtitle("Train") + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, col = "red")

p1 <- ggplot(test_pred, aes(x = observed, y = est)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  ggtitle("Test") + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, col = "red")

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p0, p1)

# Compute RMSE
rmse_train <- rmse(training_pred$observed, training_pred$est)
rmse_test <- rmse(test_pred$observed, test_pred$est)

# Compute Range
range_train <- range(train_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)
range_test <- range(test_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)

# Compute Standard Deviation
sd_train <- sd(train_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)
sd_test <- sd(test_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)

# Assemble the table
summary_table <- data.frame(
  Dataset = c("Training", "Test"),
  RMSE = c(rmse_train, rmse_test),
  Range_Min = c(range_train[1], range_test[1]),
  Range_Max = c(range_train[2], range_test[2]),
  Std_Dev = c(sd_train, sd_test)
)

# Print the summary table
print(summary_table)
##    Dataset      RMSE Range_Min Range_Max   Std_Dev
## 1 Training 1.2791456         0 31.976570 1.2961276
## 2     Test 0.6887247         0  7.145901 0.6932196

tem&salinity with ar1

# Create mesh using training data
mesh_train <- make_mesh(train_data,
                        xy_cols = c("X", "Y"),
                        cutoff = 5)

# Fit spatiotemporal model
fit_spatiotemporal <- sdmTMB(
  d_scaled ~ temperature_depth_55_std + salinity_depth_55_std,
  data = train_data, 
  mesh = mesh_train,
  time = "season",
  family = delta_gamma(link1 = "cloglog", link2 = "log"), 
  spatial = "on", 
  spatiotemporal = "ar1",
  extra_time = unique(test_data$season)
)
## Detected irregular time spacing with an AR(1) or random walk process.
## Consider filling in the missing time slices with `extra_time`.
## `extra_time = c(2018, 2020, 2021, 2022)`
## Warning in sqrt(diag(cov)): NaNs produced
## Warning: The model may not have converged: non-positive-definite Hessian
## matrix.
# Extract coefficients and sanity check
#tidy(fit_spatiotemporal, conf.int = TRUE)
sanity(fit_spatiotemporal)
## ✔ Non-linear minimizer suggests successful convergence
## ✖ Non-positive-definite Hessian matrix: model may not have converged
## ℹ Try simplifying the model, adjusting the mesh, or adding priors
## ✔ No extreme or very small eigenvalues detected
## ✔ No gradients with respect to fixed effects are >= 0.001
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## ✔ No fixed-effect standard errors are NA
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## ✖ `ln_tau_O` standard error may be large
## ℹ `ln_tau_O` is an internal parameter affecting `sigma_O`
## ℹ `sigma_O` is the spatial standard deviation
## ℹ Try simplifying the model, adjusting the mesh, or adding priors
## ✖ `ar1_phi` standard error may be large
## ℹ Try simplifying the model, adjusting the mesh, or adding priors
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## ✖ `sigma_O` is smaller than 0.01
## ℹ Consider omitting this part of the model
## ✖ `sigma_E` is smaller than 0.01
## ℹ Consider omitting this part of the model
## ✔ Range parameters don't look unreasonably large
# Predictions on training and test data
training_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = train_data, type = "response")
training_pred$observed <- train_data$d_scaled

test_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
test_pred$observed <- test_data$d_scaled

# Plotting the predictions
p0 <- ggplot(training_pred, aes(x = observed, y = est)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  ggtitle("Train") + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, col = "red")

p1 <- ggplot(test_pred, aes(x = observed, y = est)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  ggtitle("Test") + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, col = "red")

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p0, p1)

# Compute RMSE
rmse_train <- rmse(training_pred$observed, training_pred$est)
rmse_test <- rmse(test_pred$observed, test_pred$est)

# Compute Range
range_train <- range(train_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)
range_test <- range(test_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)

# Compute Standard Deviation
sd_train <- sd(train_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)
sd_test <- sd(test_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)

# Assemble the table
summary_table <- data.frame(
  Dataset = c("Training", "Test"),
  RMSE = c(rmse_train, rmse_test),
  Range_Min = c(range_train[1], range_test[1]),
  Range_Max = c(range_train[2], range_test[2]),
  Std_Dev = c(sd_train, sd_test)
)

# Print the summary table
print(summary_table)
##    Dataset      RMSE Range_Min Range_Max   Std_Dev
## 1 Training 0.7246268         0 31.976570 1.2961276
## 2     Test 0.6989406         0  7.145901 0.6932196

tem&mag with iid

# Create mesh using training data
mesh_train <- make_mesh(train_data,
                        xy_cols = c("X", "Y"),
                        cutoff = 5)

# Fit spatiotemporal model
fit_spatiotemporal <- sdmTMB(
  d_scaled ~ temperature_depth_55_std + magnitude_depth_55_std,
  data = train_data, 
  mesh = mesh_train,
  time = "season",
  family = delta_gamma(link1 = "logit", link2 = "log"), 
  spatial = "on", 
  spatiotemporal = "iid",
  extra_time = unique(test_data$season)
)
## Warning in sqrt(diag(cov)): NaNs produced
## Warning: The model may not have converged: non-positive-definite Hessian
## matrix.
# Extract coefficients and sanity check
#tidy(fit_spatiotemporal, conf.int = TRUE)
sanity(fit_spatiotemporal)
## ✔ Non-linear minimizer suggests successful convergence
## ✖ Non-positive-definite Hessian matrix: model may not have converged
## ℹ Try simplifying the model, adjusting the mesh, or adding priors
## ✔ No extreme or very small eigenvalues detected
## ✔ No gradients with respect to fixed effects are >= 0.001
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## ✔ No fixed-effect standard errors are NA
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## ✖ `ln_tau_E` standard error may be large
## ℹ `ln_tau_E` is an internal parameter affecting `sigma_E`
## ℹ `sigma_E` is the spatiotemporal standard deviation
## ℹ Try simplifying the model, adjusting the mesh, or adding priors
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## ✖ `sigma_O` is smaller than 0.01
## ℹ Consider omitting this part of the model
## ✖ `sigma_E` is smaller than 0.01
## ℹ Consider omitting this part of the model
## ✖ `sigma_E` is larger than 100
## ℹ Consider simplifying the model or adding priors
## ✔ Range parameters don't look unreasonably large
# Predictions on training and test data
training_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = train_data, type = "response")
training_pred$observed <- train_data$d_scaled

test_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
test_pred$observed <- test_data$d_scaled

# Plotting the predictions
p0 <- ggplot(training_pred, aes(x = observed, y = est)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  ggtitle("Train") + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, col = "red")

p1 <- ggplot(test_pred, aes(x = observed, y = est)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  ggtitle("Test") + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, col = "red")

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p0, p1)

# Compute RMSE
rmse_train <- rmse(training_pred$observed, training_pred$est)
rmse_test <- rmse(test_pred$observed, test_pred$est)

# Compute Range
range_train <- range(train_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)
range_test <- range(test_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)

# Compute Standard Deviation
sd_train <- sd(train_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)
sd_test <- sd(test_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)

# Assemble the table
summary_table <- data.frame(
  Dataset = c("Training", "Test"),
  RMSE = c(rmse_train, rmse_test),
  Range_Min = c(range_train[1], range_test[1]),
  Range_Max = c(range_train[2], range_test[2]),
  Std_Dev = c(sd_train, sd_test)
)

# Print the summary table
print(summary_table)
##    Dataset      RMSE Range_Min Range_Max   Std_Dev
## 1 Training 0.7281853         0 31.976570 1.2961276
## 2     Test 0.6989451         0  7.145901 0.6932196

tem&mag with rw

# Create mesh using training data
mesh_train <- make_mesh(train_data,
                        xy_cols = c("X", "Y"),
                        cutoff = 5)

# Fit spatiotemporal model
fit_spatiotemporal <- sdmTMB(
  d_scaled ~ temperature_depth_55_std + magnitude_depth_55_std,
  data = train_data, 
  mesh = mesh_train,
  time = "season",
  family = delta_gamma(link1 = "logit", link2 = "log"), 
  spatial = "on", 
  spatiotemporal = "rw",
  extra_time = unique(test_data$season)
)
## Detected irregular time spacing with an AR(1) or random walk process.
## Consider filling in the missing time slices with `extra_time`.
## `extra_time = c(2018, 2020, 2021, 2022)`
## Warning in sqrt(diag(cov)): NaNs produced
## Warning: The model may not have converged: non-positive-definite Hessian
## matrix.
# Extract coefficients and sanity check
#tidy(fit_spatiotemporal, conf.int = TRUE)
sanity(fit_spatiotemporal)
## ✔ Non-linear minimizer suggests successful convergence
## ✖ Non-positive-definite Hessian matrix: model may not have converged
## ℹ Try simplifying the model, adjusting the mesh, or adding priors
## ✔ No extreme or very small eigenvalues detected
## ✔ No gradients with respect to fixed effects are >= 0.001

## ✔ Range parameters don't look unreasonably large
# Predictions on training and test data
training_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = train_data, type = "response")
training_pred$observed <- train_data$d_scaled

test_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
test_pred$observed <- test_data$d_scaled

# Plotting the predictions
p0 <- ggplot(training_pred, aes(x = observed, y = est)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  ggtitle("Train") + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, col = "red")

p1 <- ggplot(test_pred, aes(x = observed, y = est)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  ggtitle("Test") + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, col = "red")

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p0, p1)

# Compute RMSE
rmse_train <- rmse(training_pred$observed, training_pred$est)
rmse_test <- rmse(test_pred$observed, test_pred$est)

# Compute Range
range_train <- range(train_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)
range_test <- range(test_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)

# Compute Standard Deviation
sd_train <- sd(train_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)
sd_test <- sd(test_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)

# Assemble the table
summary_table <- data.frame(
  Dataset = c("Training", "Test"),
  RMSE = c(rmse_train, rmse_test),
  Range_Min = c(range_train[1], range_test[1]),
  Range_Max = c(range_train[2], range_test[2]),
  Std_Dev = c(sd_train, sd_test)
)

# Print the summary table
print(summary_table)
##    Dataset      RMSE Range_Min Range_Max   Std_Dev
## 1 Training 1.2834990         0 31.976570 1.2961276
## 2     Test 0.6874915         0  7.145901 0.6932196

tem&mag with ar1

# Create mesh using training data
mesh_train <- make_mesh(train_data,
                        xy_cols = c("X", "Y"),
                        cutoff = 5)

# Fit spatiotemporal model
fit_spatiotemporal <- sdmTMB(
  d_scaled ~ temperature_depth_55_std + magnitude_depth_55_std,
  data = train_data, 
  mesh = mesh_train,
  time = "season",
  family = delta_gamma(link1 = "logit", link2 = "log"), 
  spatial = "on", 
  spatiotemporal = "ar1",
  extra_time = unique(test_data$season)
)
## Detected irregular time spacing with an AR(1) or random walk process.
## Consider filling in the missing time slices with `extra_time`.
## `extra_time = c(2018, 2020, 2021, 2022)`
## Warning in sqrt(diag(cov)): NaNs produced
## Warning: The model may not have converged: non-positive-definite Hessian
## matrix.
# Extract coefficients and sanity check
#tidy(fit_spatiotemporal, conf.int = TRUE)
sanity(fit_spatiotemporal)
## ✔ Non-linear minimizer suggests successful convergence
## ✖ Non-positive-definite Hessian matrix: model may not have converged
## ℹ Try simplifying the model, adjusting the mesh, or adding priors
## ✔ No extreme or very small eigenvalues detected
## ✔ No gradients with respect to fixed effects are >= 0.001
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## ✔ No fixed-effect standard errors are NA
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## ✖ `ar1_phi` standard error may be large
## ℹ Try simplifying the model, adjusting the mesh, or adding priors
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## ✖ `sigma_E` is smaller than 0.01
## ℹ Consider omitting this part of the model
## ✖ `sigma_E` is larger than 100
## ℹ Consider simplifying the model or adding priors
## ✔ Range parameters don't look unreasonably large
# Predictions on training and test data
training_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = train_data, type = "response")
training_pred$observed <- train_data$d_scaled

test_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
test_pred$observed <- test_data$d_scaled

# Plotting the predictions
p0 <- ggplot(training_pred, aes(x = observed, y = est)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  ggtitle("Train") + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, col = "red")

p1 <- ggplot(test_pred, aes(x = observed, y = est)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  ggtitle("Test") + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, col = "red")

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p0, p1)

# Compute RMSE
rmse_train <- rmse(training_pred$observed, training_pred$est)
rmse_test <- rmse(test_pred$observed, test_pred$est)

# Compute Range
range_train <- range(train_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)
range_test <- range(test_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)

# Compute Standard Deviation
sd_train <- sd(train_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)
sd_test <- sd(test_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)

# Assemble the table
summary_table <- data.frame(
  Dataset = c("Training", "Test"),
  RMSE = c(rmse_train, rmse_test),
  Range_Min = c(range_train[1], range_test[1]),
  Range_Max = c(range_train[2], range_test[2]),
  Std_Dev = c(sd_train, sd_test)
)

# Print the summary table
print(summary_table)
##    Dataset      RMSE Range_Min Range_Max   Std_Dev
## 1 Training 0.7281430         0 31.976570 1.2961276
## 2     Test 0.6989451         0  7.145901 0.6932196

lon&lat&depth55 with iid

# Create mesh using training data
mesh_train <- make_mesh(train_data,
                        xy_cols = c("X", "Y"),
                        cutoff = 10)

# Fit spatiotemporal model
fit_spatiotemporal <- sdmTMB(
  d_scaled ~ lat + lon + temperature_depth_55_std + salinity_depth_55_std + magnitude_depth_55_std + theta_depth_55_std,  
  data = train_data, 
  mesh = mesh_train,
  time = "season",
  family = delta_gamma(link1 = "logit", link2 = "log"), 
  spatial = "on", 
  spatiotemporal = "iid",
  extra_time = unique(test_data$season)
)
## Warning: The model may not have converged. Maximum final gradient:
## 0.0357983955758741.
# Extract coefficients and sanity check
#tidy(fit_spatiotemporal, conf.int = TRUE)
sanity(fit_spatiotemporal)
## ✔ Non-linear minimizer suggests successful convergence
## ✔ Hessian matrix is positive definite
## ✔ No extreme or very small eigenvalues detected
## ✖ `b_j` gradient > 0.001
## ℹ See ?run_extra_optimization(), standardize covariates, and/or simplify the model
## ✖ `b_j` gradient > 0.001
## ℹ See ?run_extra_optimization(), standardize covariates, and/or simplify the model
## ✔ No fixed-effect standard errors are NA
## ✖ `ln_tau_O` standard error may be large
## ℹ `ln_tau_O` is an internal parameter affecting `sigma_O`
## ℹ `sigma_O` is the spatial standard deviation
## ℹ Try simplifying the model, adjusting the mesh, or adding priors
## ✖ `ln_tau_E` standard error may be large
## ℹ `ln_tau_E` is an internal parameter affecting `sigma_E`
## ℹ `sigma_E` is the spatiotemporal standard deviation
## ℹ Try simplifying the model, adjusting the mesh, or adding priors
## ✖ `ln_kappa` standard error may be large
## ℹ `ln_kappa` is an internal parameter affecting `range`
## ℹ `range` is the distance at which data are effectively independent
## ℹ Try simplifying the model, adjusting the mesh, or adding priors
## ✖ `sigma_E` is smaller than 0.01
## ℹ Consider omitting this part of the model
## ✔ Range parameters don't look unreasonably large
# Predictions on training and test data
training_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = train_data, type = "response")
training_pred$observed <- train_data$d_scaled

test_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
test_pred$observed <- test_data$d_scaled

# Plotting the predictions
p0 <- ggplot(training_pred, aes(x = observed, y = est)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  ggtitle("Train") + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, col = "red")

p1 <- ggplot(test_pred, aes(x = observed, y = est)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  ggtitle("Test") + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, col = "red")

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p0, p1)

# Compute RMSE
rmse_train <- rmse(training_pred$observed, training_pred$est)
rmse_test <- rmse(test_pred$observed, test_pred$est)

# Compute Range
range_train <- range(train_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)
range_test <- range(test_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)

# Compute Standard Deviation
sd_train <- sd(train_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)
sd_test <- sd(test_data$d_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)

# Assemble the table
summary_table <- data.frame(
  Dataset = c("Training", "Test"),
  RMSE = c(rmse_train, rmse_test),
  Range_Min = c(range_train[1], range_test[1]),
  Range_Max = c(range_train[2], range_test[2]),
  Std_Dev = c(sd_train, sd_test)
)

# Print the summary table
print(summary_table)
##    Dataset      RMSE Range_Min Range_Max   Std_Dev
## 1 Training 1.2531253         0 31.976570 1.2961276
## 2     Test 0.6775498         0  7.145901 0.6932196
